message:
feat(tools,db,cli,server): shared malformed-JSON sentinels + synthetic-project-id tolerance across tools/doctor/TUI

Consolidate the "tolerate malformed attachments_json /
recipients_json" parsing across every surface that reads inbox
rows — previously each crate had its own open-coded
`serde_json::from_str(...).unwrap_or_default()` call — and extend
the synthetic-project-id tolerance pattern (committed earlier in
this batch for mail status, projects adopt, TUI compose, and
contacts) to the doctor-check and TUI message-search paths that
were still doing bare `SELECT id FROM projects WHERE slug = ?`
lookups.

== Shared malformed-JSON parsing helpers ==

crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/lib.rs

- tool_util module: add two sentinel constants and a shared
  parsing surface used by every tool that materializes an
  InboxRow into a JSON payload:
    const MALFORMED_ATTACHMENTS_SENTINEL = "[malformed-attachments-json]"
    const MALFORMED_RECIPIENTS_SENTINEL  = "[malformed-recipients-json]"

- New struct ParsedRecipients { to, cc, bcc } — the canonical
  Rust representation of the `{"to": [...], "cc": [...], "bcc":
  [...]}` shape recipients_json carries.  Derives Default,
  PartialEq, Eq, and serde::Deserialize so callers can build it
  directly from a JSON Value.

- New fn is_valid_recipients_payload(value) -> bool validates
  that the top-level JSON object has to/cc/bcc arrays of
  strings; anything else is treated as malformed.  This catches
  subtle corruption like `{"to": "WindyGate"}` (string instead
  of array) that serde_json::from_str would happily accept
  into a Value but that ParsedRecipients::deserialize would
  silently drop.

- New fn parse_attachment_metadata_json(input) ->
  Vec<serde_json::Value>:
    empty trim → Vec::new()
    valid JSON array → the array
    anything else → single-element sentinel payload
        [{"name": MALFORMED_ATTACHMENTS_SENTINEL, "media_type":
          null, "path": null, "bytes": null}]
  The sentinel preserves a detectable signal for downstream
  consumers that want to flag rows for repair.

- New fn parse_recipients_json_value(input) -> serde_json::Value:
    empty → json!({})
    valid + valid-shape → the parsed Value
    invalid shape OR parse error → malformed_recipients_payload
        json!({"to": [MALFORMED_RECIPIENTS_SENTINEL], "cc": [],
               "bcc": []})

- New fn parse_recipients_lists(input) -> ParsedRecipients:
  feeds parse_recipients_json_value through serde_json::from_value
  into the ParsedRecipients struct.  On any failure, returns a
  ParsedRecipients with the sentinel in `to` and empty cc/bcc,
  so callers see a well-formed struct even for broken rows.

- Unit tests:
    parse_attachment_metadata_json_surfaces_malformed_payloads —
        covers empty + broken.
    parse_recipients_lists_surfaces_malformed_payloads —
        covers empty, shape-invalid ({"to":"string"}), and
        parse-error cases.

=== Migrate the existing call sites ===

crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/messaging.rs
- Import parse_attachment_metadata_json + parse_recipients_lists.
- Delete the inline FastRecipients struct that duplicated
  ParsedRecipients.
- fetch_inbox(): replace
    serde_json::from_str(...).unwrap_or_default()
  with the helper calls.  Four fewer call sites drop malformed
  JSON silently.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/products.rs
- Same migration in fetch_inbox_product(), plus a new integration
  test that seeds an archive row with `attachments = "{not-json}"`
  and `recipients_json = "{not a real payload}"` and asserts the
  product inbox output contains both sentinel markers.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/resources.rs
- parse_attachment_metadata and parse_recipients_json (the
  resource-layer private wrappers) now forward to
  parse_attachment_metadata_json and parse_recipients_json_value
  respectively, so every resource URL goes through the same
  malformed-payload path as the tools surface.
- New test resources_surface_malformed_message_metadata seeds a
  project with one message whose attachments/recipients JSON is
  broken, serves the resource, and asserts the rendered payload
  contains the sentinel strings.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_service.rs
- search_service previously had its own open-coded FastRecipients
  struct with the same "unwrap_or_default" behavior.  Add
  module-local copies of MALFORMED_RECIPIENTS_SENTINEL,
  is_valid_recipients_payload, and a new parse_search_result_recipients
  that applies the same shape validation.  (Cannot import from
  mcp-agent-mail-tools here because that would flip the
  dependency direction; keeping the helpers local is fine since
  they're ~40 lines.)
- Swap the bare from_str().unwrap_or_default() path to
  parse_search_result_recipients so search results carry the
  sentinel marker instead of silently dropping recipients on
  malformed rows.

== Synthetic-project-id tolerance, continued ==

crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs

- handle_doctor_check_with() was the last caller in the doctor
  surface still doing
      SELECT id, slug FROM projects WHERE slug = ?
  directly.  Route it through
      crate::context::resolve_project(&conn, slug)
  so the "project_exists" check passes for synthetic
  "[unknown-project-N]" slugs that have recoverable agents/
  messages rows even though the projects row was wiped.
- The follow-up agent-count query now keys on the resolved
  project.id instead of joining through projects.slug.
- New test
  doctor_check_project_specific_checks_accept_orphaned_project_placeholder
  exercises the exact scenario: insert a project row, delete it,
  seed dependent agents/messages, then run doctor check against
  the placeholder slug and assert the agent-count sub-check
  still succeeds.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/messages.rs

- project_id_for_slug(): check synthetic_project_id(slug) first
  and return its parsed id when present, before falling back to
  the `SELECT id FROM projects WHERE slug = ?` lookup.  Mirrors
  the same early-return pattern already used by the other TUI
  paths in this file.
- New test fetch_recent_messages_accepts_placeholder_project_identifier
  builds an in-memory DB with agents/projects/messages schemas,
  inserts a message at project_id=1, then drops the projects
  row and fetches recent messages by the "[unknown-project-1]"
  slug to prove the synthetic id path works through the full
  message fetch pipeline.

Together this closes out the "tolerate orphaned project metadata
+ malformed JSON payloads" work across every tool surface.
After this commit, the three patterns — tool inbox, doctor
check, TUI message search — all share the same code paths for
placeholder project slugs and malformed row payloads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
